# FP-43553: [Competitions] Future tournaments carry pre-release ConfigJson generated by old binary

## Status
Completed 2026-05-12. Steam prod was hot-patched 2026-04-29; second-round `MaxRating` defect mitigated the same evening. PS / XB / MOB / NX rollouts completed by DevOps on 2026-05-11. Release procedure codified: Server Release Checklist template gained a conditional `TournamentJsonConfig`-extension step, developer-side obligation in `<kb>/feedback/configjson_extension_backfill.md`. Code-side root-cause fix tracked separately as FP-43717. Operator tooling tracked as FP-43758 (non-destructive admin action) and FP-43756 (cancel competitions during release downtime — Proposal to GD). Remaining items either split out into the above tickets, or explicitly deferred-skipped per GD lead / explicit decision (see backlog Deferred / Notes).

## Summary
Post-deploy of the Leaderboards/Rating/Matchmaking release on Steam revealed 7 already-scheduled Competition tournaments (`Tournaments.KindId = 3`) with `ConfigJson` lacking the new fields `Places[i].Rating`, `NoShowRatingPenalty`, `ZeroScoreRatingPenalty`, and `Grouping`. Root cause: `ScheduleCompetitions` / `RandomizeCompetitions` (`Shared/SharedLib/Tournaments/TournamentSchedulingAdapter.cs`) generate each Tournament by deserialising the source template's `ConfigJson` into the C# `TournamentJsonConfig` model and re-serialising it; properties absent from the model at generation time are silently dropped, so any Tournament generated by the previous binary stays frozen on the old field set. `RegenerateFutureCompetitions` cannot heal them once registrations exist (fixed 2-hour `RegistrationStart` cutoff), and `RegenerateCompetitionsRandom` would cascade away participant data — neither standard flow was acceptable.

While diagnosing, two adjacent issues surfaced in templates: (B) `Place 7` of template 181 had no `Rating` (a content defect that any tournament generated from the template inherits); (C) 15 active templates carry a `FinishPoint` block written as a JavaScript object literal with unquoted X/Y/Z property names — accepted by Newtonsoft (lenient by default) but rejected by SQL Server's strict JSON functions, breaking diagnostic and patch tooling.

All hand-crafted SQL from sections A–D of the audit trail has been distilled into the idempotent fix-up script `SQL/Releases/R202604-Leaderboards-Rating-Matchmaking-FixUp.sql` — see audit trail section E for what it automates, what it intentionally does not, and the local smoke-test outcome (validated end-to-end). Incident details mirrored in `modules/matchmaking/log.md` entry 2026-04-29.

## Plan
No formal phasing — work driven by issues as they surface. Open threads (also tracked granularly in [backlog](backlog.md)):

> Audit trail of the Steam prod hot-patch — concrete TournamentIds, source-template values, exact SQL blocks (ratings backfill, penalty backfill, `Grouping` copy, template-181 / tournament-320415 Place-7 fix, 15-template FinishPoint normalisation, second-round `Brackets[i].MaxRating` backfill), and post-patch verification: [artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md](artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md).

- Code-side root-cause fix for the `MaxRating` implicit contract: tracked under FP-43717 (eliminate persisted `MaxRating` dependency)
- Operator tooling: tracked under FP-43756 (cancel competitions overlapping release downtime — Proposal to GD) and FP-43758 (non-destructive `RefreshFutureCompetitionConfigs` admin action, including typo/naming/constants cleanup)
- Scheduling-adapter unknown-fields refactor: deferred-skipped per explicit decision — see [backlog](backlog.md) Deferred / Notes
- `JsonVerificator.exe` strict-parse and `Missions`/`TargetedAds` `ISJSON` audit: deferred-skipped per GD lead — see [backlog](backlog.md) Deferred / Notes

## Milestones
- 2026-04-29: Diagnosis on Steam prod — phase summary surfaced 7 affected future Competition tournaments (1 live, 6 registration-open), all with `Places[i].Rating`/`NoShowRatingPenalty`/`ZeroScoreRatingPenalty`/`Grouping` missing. 268 distinct registered users would have received `0` rating delta and skipped bracket assignment. `RegenerateFutureCompetitions` ruled out — `RegistrationStart` already in the past for all seven. Root cause traced to deserialize→reserialize cycle in `TournamentSchedulingAdapter`
- 2026-04-29: Steam prod fix — backfilled `Places[i].Rating` (places 1..10) and root-level `NoShowRatingPenalty` / `ZeroScoreRatingPenalty` for all 7 tournaments via point `JSON_MODIFY`, values copied from each Tournament's source template
- 2026-04-29: Steam prod fix — backfilled `Grouping` for the 6 upcoming tournaments. Live tournament left as-is: `ProcessGrouping` had already run (no `BracketId` to assign retroactively) and `RatingMultiplier = 1.0` in the source template makes rating math identical with or without `Grouping` in the live config
- 2026-04-29: Surfaced content class C — 15 active competition templates have RFC-invalid `FinishPoint` JSON (unquoted X/Y/Z in `Position` / `Rotation` / `Scale`). Investigated WebAdmin: `JsonVerificator.exe` and `DataEditingModels.DeserializeJson` both rely on `JsonConvert.DeserializeObject` (Newtonsoft default lenient), which accepts unquoted property names. Server-side generation also goes through Newtonsoft and silently normalises on serialise, so `Tournaments.ConfigJson` is RFC-valid even when the source template is not. SQL Server `ISJSON` / `OPENJSON` are strict and reject template directly. Normalised the 15 templates via idempotent `REPLACE` (`{ X:` → `{ "X":`, `, Y:` → `, "Y":`, `, Z:` → `, "Z":`)
- 2026-04-29: Surfaced content class B — template 181 ("Triple the Trout!") had `Place 7` with no `Rating` (other places: 35 / 30 / 25 / 22 / 19 / 15 / ? / 11 / 7 / 3). Design provided `Rating = 13`. Backfilled in template 181 and in the only in-horizon Tournament generated from it (`320415`, scheduled 2026-05-06)
- 2026-04-29: Created diagnostic SQL `SQL/Releases/R202604-Leaderboards-Rating-Matchmaking.sql` for repeated use across platforms. Five queries: phase summary (Q1), per-tournament detail with `arch`/`content`/`mixed`/`tpl-invalid` verdicts (Q2), template-side content defects on rating fields (Q3), root-level key diff Templates ↔ Tournaments with whitelist of expected deviations (Q4), templates/tournaments with malformed JSON (Q5). Hardened against malformed-JSON rows via `ISJSON = 1` filters and `CASE`-wrapped `OPENJSON`. All five queries verified on Steam prod returning clean state
- 2026-04-29: Recorded incident root-cause analysis in `modules/matchmaking/log.md` entry 2026-04-29 — both the architectural class and the lesson about release procedure for `TournamentJsonConfig` extensions
- 2026-04-29: Filed JIRA FP-43553 (Bug, High, Severity major, Platform Steam, Scrum Team Leaderboards, Fix Version `2026.3.1. Leaderboards hotfix`); JIRA auto-linked it under parent Epic FP-41583 (this Epic is "Prod Bugs_2026 (All Platforms)" — the common 2026 prod-bugs umbrella across all platforms, not a release-specific Epic). KB journal created (this file)
- 2026-04-29: Compiled audit trail of concrete IDs / values / transactional SQL blocks in [artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md](artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md). Same patterns are applicable verbatim to PS / XB / MOB / NX, but the values must be re-derived from each platform's own `TournamentTemplates`
- 2026-04-29 (evening): Diagnosis — matchmaking on 318868 (ended), 318869 (ended), 318870 (live) reported only 35–45 % of registered participants were assigned to a single group A in bracket 1, the rest left with `BracketId IS NULL`, `GroupName IS NULL`. Traced to section A.4 of the audit trail: `JSON_QUERY(tt.ConfigJson, '$.Grouping')` returns the template's `Grouping` without `Brackets[i].MaxRating` (templates never store it — auto-computed by `MatchmakingLogic.InitializeGrouping` during template deserialization), and `TournamentsHelper.FromDto(TournamentDto)` does not invoke that initializer at competition start. With `int MaxRating` defaulting to 0, `MatchmakingLogic.CreateBuckets` admitted only `CompetitionRating == 0` (incl. `NULL` mapped to 0) into bucket 1 and produced inverted-empty ranges `[101..0]` / `[1001..0]` for buckets 2 and 3. The regular generation pipeline (`ScheduleCompetitions` / `RandomizeCompetitions`) sidesteps the asymmetry by deserializing the template first and re-serializing the now-initialized `Grouping` into `Tournaments.ConfigJson`, so all `TournamentId >= 320342` rows are healthy by construction. [Matchmaking spec on Confluence](https://fishingplanet.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/FP/pages/5505613835) confirms `MaxRating` is auto-computed and "do not set manually" — formalises the implicit contract this defect class breaks
- 2026-04-29 (evening): Steam prod fix — backfilled `Grouping.Brackets[i].MaxRating` (100 / 1000 / 2147483647 — recomputed from each bracket's `MinRating[i+1] - 1` rule, matches what `InitializeGrouping` would compute) for the two still-upcoming Competition tournaments 318871 and 318872 via point `JSON_MODIFY`, applied >5 minutes before each `StartDate` so the AsyncProcessor `GetTournamentsToStartSoon` re-poll picks up the patched ConfigJson before `ProcessGrouping` runs (no Tournament-DTO-level cache exists; `TournamentsCache` only covers `TournamentSeries`). The three already-running tournaments (318868 ended, 318869 ended, 318870 live) left as-is — `ProcessGrouping` had already executed and `RatingMultiplier = 1.0` on every bracket makes per-bracket rating math equivalent. `MaxRating`-aware phase summary post-patch: 138 far-future + 1 reg-opens-soon + 6 reg-open + 1 live; only the deliberate 318870 row reports `GroupingPresentButMaxMissing > 0`. Audit trail extended with section D in `artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md`
- 2026-05-06: Distilled all hand-crafted SQL from sections A–D of the Steam audit trail into one idempotent fix-up script: `SQL/Releases/R202604-Leaderboards-Rating-Matchmaking-FixUp.sql`. Single batch, three execution phases (Phase 0 normalises `FinishPoint` JSON in any template whose `ConfigJson LIKE '%FinishPoint%' AND ISJSON = 0` — no `KindId` filter, the JSON malformedness is platform-wide; Phase 1 read-only sanity gate scoped to `KindId = 3`, aborts on template content defects, live tournaments, or tournaments inside the AsyncProcessor 5-minute window; Phase 2 backfills `Grouping` + per-bracket `MaxRating` + `Places[i].Rating` 1..10 + both `*RatingPenalty` fields, with idempotency guards on every UPDATE; Phase 3 verify-before-commit gate refuses to persist if any `Missing*` is non-zero). Default `@DryRun = 1` — outer transaction wraps Phase 0 + Phase 2 / 3 so a smoke run leaves the database byte-identical. Strictly `KindId = 3` for the patchable cohort, every read with `WITH (NOLOCK)`. Validated end-to-end on the local development DB (15 templates normalised, 1 template content defect surfaced and manually fixed, 2 inheritance-defect tournaments backfilled, Phase 3 verification clean, COMMIT). Audit trail extended with section E describing what the script automates and what it intentionally does not (already-grouped tournaments and the upstream code-side asymmetry). The diagnostic Q1..Q5 queries that were authored during investigation lived only in the working copy; their functionality is fully absorbed by the fix-up script's Phase 1 + Phase 3 end-to-end
- 2026-05-11: PS / XB / MOB / NX prod rollouts completed by DevOps as part of platform release checklists. Release procedure codified: Server Release Checklist template (Confluence page 4395597825, version 17) gained a new conditional step "Fix generated competitions" placed immediately after `Regenerate future competitive activities`, conditional on `TournamentJsonConfig` / `TournamentTemplateJsonConfig` being extended, with inline dry-run → review → commit procedure. Developer-side obligation captured in `<kb>/feedback/jsonconfig_extension_backfill.md` — any PR extending a `*JsonConfig` schema persisted into pre-generated rows must ship an idempotent back-fill SQL script in `SQL/Releases/`. Backlog items #1–4 (platform rollouts), #5 (release procedure), #7 (periodic content audit subsumed by template step) closed
- 2026-05-12: Closure prep — three remaining backlog threads resolved. Scheduling-adapter unknown-fields refactor (item #8) deferred-skipped: with FP-43758 providing a non-destructive operator action that refreshes ConfigJson from templates, the only failure mode would be bypassing the canonical pipeline; defence-in-depth value does not justify the architectural change. `JsonVerificator.exe` strict-parse (item #6) and adjacent `Missions`/`TargetedAds` `ISJSON` audit (item #10) deferred-skipped per GD lead: designers rely on the current lenient JSON syntax across templates and adjacent content columns; SQL-side normalisation already covers the deploy-time risk. Split-outs: B-1 (Upcoming Release page kind=3 cancellation) → FP-43756 (Proposal to GD); B-2 (RefreshFutureCompetitionConfigs admin action) → FP-43758, scope includes typo/naming/constants cleanup. KB feedback file renamed `jsonconfig_extension_backfill.md` → `configjson_extension_backfill.md` for column-centric framing. Memory pointer persisted for post-FP-43758 rewrite of the renamed feedback rule (intentionally not in FP-43758 scope)
